old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No animal-husbandry dependency remains because the source identifies animal-fat combustible but does not identify domesticated animals or managed herds as its source.

source_shape.source_locator: Lighting section, paragraph 2: analysis of soot in the recognized sandstone lamp identifies animal-fat combustible without specifying domestication.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Animal fat is evidenced as lamp fuel, but managed livestock is not evidenced as the fuel source.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Wild hunted animals can supply fat, so the material identification cannot establish the much narrower husbandry capability.

ontology_before: Any animal-fat fuel was treated as evidence that domesticated-herd management enabled the lamp.
ontology_after: The lamp retains its evidenced fat fuel without converting animal material into an unsupported husbandry lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: oil_lamps no longer directly depends on animal_husbandry.
would_reject_if: Direct residue or contextual evidence established that the Lascaux lamp fuel came from managed domestic livestock.
short_reason: Animal fat does not by itself evidence animal husbandry.
